zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Brampton於1286年10月27日出生在英國賀福爾蕾托 布藍普頓時，她的父親Sir Bryan de Brampton23歲，母親Eleanor de Hereford17歲。她和Sir Robert Harley至少有2個兒子。她去世於1349年6月4日在英國史洛普爾哈爾享年62歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Brampton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from one or more of the many places called Brampton (Northants, Hunts, Herefs, Derbys, Lincs, WR Yorks, Norfolk, Suffolk, Westm, Cumb). There is a distinct early bias towards the E Midlands, E Anglia and Yorks.

variants.name: Brunton
meanings: Scottish and northern English: habitational name from Brunton in Fife, from Old English burna ‘stream’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. This surname is also common in Ireland.
English: variant of Brompton, a habitational name from any of the places called from Brompton in East and North Yorkshire, Middlesex, and Shropshire. Or, a variant of Branton 2.

variants.name: Brompton
meanings: 1  English: locative name from Brompton (ER Yorks, NR Yorks, Middx, Shrops).

variants.name: Branton
meanings: habitational name from any of the places called Branton in South Yorkshire (formerly in Yorkshire) and Northumberland or from Braunton in Devon. The first and last are named with Old English brōm ‘broom’ + tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’. The second is from an Old English word brēmen ‘overgrown with broom’ + tūn ‘farmstead’.

variant of Brampton, a habitational name from one or more of the many places called Brampton (Northamptonshire, Huntingtonshire, Herefordshire, Derbyshire, Lincolnshire, Yorkshire, Norfolk, Suffolk, Westmorland, and Cumberland), all of which are named with Old English brōm ‘broom’ + tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Brampton was born on 27 October 1286, in Little Brampton, Herefordshire, England, her father, Sir Bryan de Brampton, was 23 and her mother, Eleanor de Hereford, was 17. She had at least 2 sons with Sir Robert Harley. She died on 4 June 1349, in Harley, Shropshire, England, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Brampton est née le 27 octobre 1286, à Little Brampton, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Bryan de Brampton, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Eleanor de Hereford, avait 17 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils avec Sir Robert Harley. Elle est décédée le 4 juin 1349, à Harley, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Brampton am 27. Oktober 1286 in Little Brampton, Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Bryan de Brampton 23 und ihre Mutter Eleanor de Hereford 17. Sie hatte mit Sir Robert Harley mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb am 4. Juni 1349 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Harley, Shropshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Brampton è nata il il 27 ottobre 1286 nel Little Brampton, Herefordshire, England, sua padre, Sir Bryan de Brampton, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Eleanor de Hereford, aveva 17 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli con Sir Robert Harley. Morì il 4 giugno 1349, nel Harley, Shropshire, England, all'età di 62 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brampton(이)가 1286년 10월 27일 영국 허퍼드실르 리틀 브램프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Bryan de Brampton(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor de Hereford(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 Sir Robert Harley(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1349년 6월 4일 영국 슈럽실르 하를리에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.27 October 1286–4 June 1349: 27 de outubro de 1286–4 de junho de 1349
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Brampton nasceu em 27 de outubro de 1286, em Little Brampton, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Bryan de Brampton, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor de Hereford, tinha 17 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Sir Robert Harley. Ela faleceu em 4 de junho de 1349, em Harley, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Brampton родилась 27 октября 1286 г. в местности литл брамптон, хирефордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Bryan de Brampton было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Eleanor de Hereford, 17. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына от Sir Robert Harley. Она умерла 4 июня 1349 г. в местности харли, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 62 лет.

Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Sir Robert Harley. Murió el 4 de junio de 1349, en Harley, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 62 años.
